title: Undo & Redo
intro: `u` undoes the last change and `Ctrl-r` redoes it. Undo works in change-sized steps: a single `u` reverts an entire `dd` or a whole Insert-mode session.

exercises.instruction: You meant to delete the LAST line. Delete line 1 with `dd` — mistake! — undo with `u`, then delete the last line with `G` `dd`.
hints: One `u` restores the whole deleted line.

exercises.instruction: Delete the comment with `dd`, undo with `u`, then redo the delete with `Ctrl-r`.
hints: `Ctrl-r` re-applies the change you just undid.

exercises.instruction: Insert `hi` before the `;` (`i`, type, `Esc`) — wrong value! Undo it with `u`, then insert `hello` instead.
hints: `u` reverts the whole Insert session in one step.

exercises.instruction: Delete the top line three times in a row (`dd` ×3), then bring the LAST deletion back with a single `u`.
hints: Each `dd` is its own undo step — `u` only rewinds the most recent one.

exercises.instruction: Delete `alpha ` with `dw` — wrong word! Undo, then delete `beta ` instead (`w` `dw`).
hints: After `u` the cursor is back where the change happened.

exercises.instruction: `dd` the debug line, second-guess yourself with `u`, then commit with `Ctrl-r`.
hints: Undo and redo are a pair — flip back and forth freely.
